title: taraxacum subgentiliforme
intro: Taraxacum subgentiliforme, also known as the lesser horse's-tail dandelion, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is a perennial plant that shares similar medicinal properties with the common dandelion, Taraxacum officinale, including its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Taraxacum subgentiliforme include its ability to support kidney function, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
